content: I'm so excited to be living in Raleigh, NC now. I just moved here from Boston a few months ago, and I'm loving the warmer weather.
Raleigh is a great city! What brought you to the area, and how are you finding the adjustment to the South?

I moved here for my job as a Robotics Researcher at MIT's Raleigh lab. I'm really enjoying the work and the team, but I do miss my family and friends back in Boston. My border collie, Ziggy, is also adjusting well to the new surroundings.

It's always tough to leave behind loved ones, but it's great that you're enjoying your work and that Ziggy is settling in well. What do you like to do in your free time, and have you explored much of the Raleigh area yet?

I love trying new restaurants and breweries, and I've been getting into photography lately. I've also been exploring the local hiking trails and trying to find the best spots to watch the sunset. My colleague, Dr. Patel, has been showing me around and introducing me to some of the local hidden gems.
That sounds like a great way to spend your free time! Have you tried any of the Japanese restaurants in the area, or is there a particular type of cuisine you're craving?

I'm actually a big fan of Japanese cuisine, and I've been trying to find the best sushi spots in Raleigh. I've also been experimenting with cooking Japanese dishes at home, like ramen and udon noodles.
That's great! Cooking can be such a fun and creative outlet. Have you thought about joining any local food or photography groups to meet like-minded people?
That's a great idea! I'd love to meet other people who share my interests. I've also been thinking about taking up surfing or mountain biking to get some more exercise and enjoy the outdoors.

Those are both great activities! There are some beautiful beaches and trails in the area that would be perfect for surfing or mountain biking. Have you considered volunteering or participating in any local events to get more involved in the community?

I've been thinking about volunteering at a local animal shelter, since I love animals and want to give back to the community. I've also been brewing my own beer at home and might consider joining a local homebrew club.
That's wonderful! Volunteering and giving back to the community can be so rewarding. What kind of beer do you like to brew, and have you thought about entering any local brewing competitions?

I love brewing IPAs and stouts, and I've actually been thinking about entering a competition soon. I've got a batch of beer that's currently fermenting, and I'm excited to see how it turns out. My friend, Mike, has been giving me some great tips and advice on brewing.
